---
title: "Microclimate.jl"
subtitle: "Physics-based microclimates from points to landscapes"
author: "Rafael Schouten — Montpellier 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    width: 1280
    height: 800
    margin: 0.05
    code-line-numbers: false
    highlight-style: github
---

## What does a frog feel?

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}
Climate datasets report **air temperature at 2 m**.

Most organisms don't live there.

- A lizard on bare soil: surface T, not 2 m
- A seedling: 1 cm air T
- A burrowing mammal: soil T at 30 cm
- A bryophyte on a north-facing slope: yet another regime

The gap between *macroclimate* and what an organism actually experiences is the **microclimate**.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
![Air vs soil-surface temperature, July day at Montpellier](figures/point_temperatures.png)
:::

:::

---

## Microclimate.jl

A Julia rewrite of the physics behind [NicheMapR](https://github.com/mrke/NicheMapR) (Kearney & Porter 2017).

::: {.incremental}
- 1-D coupled soil-energy and atmospheric-profile model
- Solves heat and water transport in the soil column
- Couples to a Monin–Obukhov boundary-layer profile above
- Produces hourly air, soil, humidity, wind at user-chosen heights and depths
- Decoupled, composable Julia packages instead of monolithic Fortran
:::

---

## The package ecosystem

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 12
flowchart TB
  subgraph data["Data"]
    direction LR
    RDS[RasterDataSources.jl]
    RAS[Rasters.jl]
    NC[NCDatasets.jl]
  end

  subgraph forcing["Forcing"]
    direction LR
    SOL[SolarRadiation.jl]
    FLU[FluidProperties.jl]
    GEO[Geomorphometry.jl]
  end

  subgraph core["Physics core"]
    MIC[Microclimate.jl]
    MP[ModelParameters.jl]
    SCI[SciML / OrdinaryDiffEq]
  end

  subgraph scale["Landscape scaling"]
    BPG[BiophysicalGrids.jl]
  end

  subgraph eco["Downstream ecology"]
    direction LR
    BEH[BiophysicalBehaviour.jl]
    HEX[HeatExchange.jl]
    DEB[DynamicEnergyBudgets.jl]
    SDM["Any SDM<br/>(your model here)"]
  end

  data --> forcing
  data --> scale
  forcing --> core
  MP --> core
  SCI --> core
  core --> scale
  core --> eco
  scale --> eco

  classDef ext fill:#eef,stroke:#557
  classDef core fill:#fee,stroke:#a44
  classDef scale fill:#efe,stroke:#4a4
  class RDS,RAS,NC,SOL,FLU,GEO,MP,SCI ext
  class MIC core
  class BPG scale
```

::: {.fragment}
Each box is an independent registered Julia package. Swap one out, the rest still work.
:::

---

## A microclimate at a point

```julia
using BiophysicalGrids, Microclimate, FluidProperties, Unitful

lon, lat  = 3.8772, 43.6108           # Montpellier
elevation = 27.0u"m"

weather = get_weather(TerraClimate, lon, lat;
    ystart = 2000,
    elevation,
    vapour_pressure_method = GoffGratch(),
)

site = Site(;
    latitude  = lat * u"°",  longitude = lon * u"°",  elevation,
    slope     = 0.0u"°",     aspect    = 0.0u"°",
    horizon_angles    = fill(0.0u"°", 24),
    sky_view_fraction = 1.0,
    albedo            = 0.15,
    roughness_height  = 0.004u"m",
    atmospheric_pressure = atmospheric_pressure(elevation),
)

result = simulate_microclimate(site, soil_thermal, soil_hydraulics, weather)
```

::: {.fragment}
That's the whole API. Three calls.
:::

---

## Output: a July day in Montpellier

![](figures/point_temperatures.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

Soil surface swings ≈ 30 °C diurnally — 2 m air swings ≈ 12 °C.

---

## Scaling up: BiophysicalGrids.jl

Per-pixel `simulate_microclimate` over a DEM.

```julia
using BiophysicalGrids, Rasters, ArchGDAL, Geomorphometry

# 1. SRTM DEM, reprojected to UTM
(; utm_dem, x_coords_utm, y_coords_utm, nx_utm, ny_utm) =
    load_utm_dem(center_lon, center_lat, extent_lon, extent_lat)

# 2. Slope, aspect, horizon angles per pixel
(; elevation_m, slope_deg, aspect_deg, horizons_u, pressure_r, ...) =
    compute_terrain_grids(utm_dem, x_coords_utm, y_coords_utm)

# 3. TerraClimate at centre, lapse-correct per pixel
weather = get_weather(TerraClimate, center_lon, center_lat; ystart = 2000)

# 4. Loop pixels (threaded), warm-start each row from the row above
Threads.@threads for j in 1:nx_utm
    site_ij = Site(; elevation = elevation_m[i,j], slope = slope_deg[i,j], ...)
    result  = simulate_microclimate(site_ij, soil_thermal, soil_hydraulics, wp_ij)
end
```

---

## Mont Aigoual, Cévennes — July 2000

Soil-surface temperature, 6 hours of the diurnal cycle.

![](figures/grid_Tsoil0.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

::: {.fragment}
North-facing slopes stay cool past noon. South-facing rocks reach 50 °C.
:::

---

## Air temperature at 1 cm — animated

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](figures/grid_Tair1cm.gif){fig-align="center"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
- 70 × 70 SRTM pixels
- 24 hourly snapshots
- Same `simulate_microclimate` call as the point case
- Threaded across columns, warm-started across rows
- ~minutes on a laptop
:::

:::

---

## Air at 2 m vs soil surface

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Air, 2 m** — what TerraClimate gives you.

![](figures/grid_Tair2m.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Soil surface** — what a lizard feels.

![](figures/grid_Tsoil0.png)
:::

:::

The map you need depends on the organism. Both come from the same model run.

---

## What you put on top is up to you

`MicroResult` is a struct of hourly air, soil, humidity, wind profiles. It is forcing for *any* organism-level model:

::: {.incremental}
- **Heat budgets** — operative temperature, ectotherm activity windows (`HeatExchange.jl`, `BiophysicalBehaviour.jl`)
- **Dynamic Energy Budget** — growth, reproduction, survival (`DynamicEnergyBudgets.jl`)
- **Species distribution models** — replace bioclim layers with mechanistic ones; correlative or process-based, your choice
- **Your model** — read from a struct, no FFI, no NetCDF gymnastics
:::

::: {.fragment}
The point of the ecosystem is: **microclimate is a service, not a destination.**
:::

---

## Status & roadmap

::: {.incremental}
- Microclimate.jl — validated against NicheMapR's monthly outputs at point scale
- BiophysicalGrids.jl — terrain + per-pixel runner with lapse correction and warm-starting
- ERA5 hourly forcing (next): drop-in `get_weather(ERA5, ...)`
- Snow, cold-air drainage, urban canopies — in progress
- Open source, MIT, contributions welcome
:::

---

## Thanks

::: {.incremental}
- **Mike Kearney** (Melbourne) — NicheMapR, biophysical ecology vision
- BiophysicalEcology contributors
- Rasters.jl, SciML, JuliaGeo ecosystems
:::

::: {.fragment}

**Code & slides**: [github.com/BiophysicalEcology](https://github.com/BiophysicalEcology)

**This talk**: pre-rendered, source open, every figure rebuilt in CI.

:::
